A while ago, a pair of researchers decided to test the influence of one particular cue of a thord, an expensive business suit. They did so by arranging for an average looking man to stand on a crowded street corner and then jaywalk into and across the street. In half of the instances, he took this risky action while wearing jeans and a t-shirt. The other half of the times, he wore a well-tailored business suit. The result. 350% more people followed behind him into the street against the light, against the traffic, against the law. This shows how the often automatic, unthinking way people react to symbols of authority can work. Even if that guy had been the high-powered business executive his clothing suggested, if those around them had thought about it, they would have realized that he wasn't anymore an authority on crossing the street than the rest of them. But that's the point. They weren't thinking. They were reacting. Reacting to the mere aura of authority.
